pull.title: doc: clarify libfuzzer-nosan preset uses build_fuzz_nosan dir
pull.body:
Adds a clarifying note next to the first mention of the `libfuzzer-nosan` preset in the Quickstart, pointing out that it uses a different build directory (`build_fuzz_nosan`, per [`CMakePresets.json` L54](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CMakePresets.json#L54)).

A reader following the quickstart with `--preset=libfuzzer-nosan` and then running `cmake --build build_fuzz` as shown would otherwise operate against the wrong (or empty) directory.

Pure docs; no code changes.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35274. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [l0rinc](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35274#issuecomment-4441162033), [maflcko](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35274#issuecomment-4444176911), [sedited](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35274#pullrequestreview-4284556674) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.
It's probably enough to mention it in a single place - concept ACK

events.message:
doc: clarify libfuzzer-nosan preset uses build_fuzz_nosan dir

The fuzzing quickstart documents the libfuzzer preset with build_fuzz
as the build directory, then mentions libfuzzer-nosan as an alternative
without noting that this preset uses a different binary directory
(build_fuzz_nosan, per CMakePresets.json line 54). A reader following
the quickstart with --preset=libfuzzer-nosan and then running
'cmake --build build_fuzz' as shown will operate against the wrong
(or empty) directory.

Add a clarifying note at the first mention in the Quickstart.

events.body:
ACK 0065f354a7e9435fbc64f2bdc7b2b695df90cf4c

~The PR description still needs to be updated~ Done

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -125,6 +127,15 @@ occasional long runs without the additional bug-detectors
 (`--preset=libfuzzer-nosan`) and then merge new inputs into a corpus as described in
 the qa-assets repo
 (https://github.com/bitcoin-core/qa-assets/blob/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md).
+The `libfuzzer-nosan` preset places its build artifacts in `build_fuzz_nosan` (not
+`build_fuzz`), so the corresponding build and run commands are:
+
+```sh
+$ cmake --preset=libfuzzer-nosan
+$ cmake --build build_fuzz_nosan
+$ FUZZ=process_message build_fuzz_nosan/bin/fuzz
+```
+
comments.body: Agree with l0rinc here, repeating this doesn't seem too useful.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -14,6 +14,8 @@ $ FUZZ=process_message build_fuzz/bin/fuzz
 ```
 
 One can use `--preset=libfuzzer-nosan` to do the same without common sanitizers enabled.
+Note that this preset uses `build_fuzz_nosan` as its build directory, so substitute
comments.body:
The `substitute` phrasing made me read it twice - so which one do I replace with which one?
```suggestion
Note that this preset uses a different build directory, so replace `build_fuzz` with `build_fuzz_nosan` in the build and run commands above.
```

You might want to mention https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/7164a0cab650bdf01cdcbc3da690f6b674fcc7b3/CMakePresets.json#L54 in the PR description or commit message (and update it since the description still mentions both previous changes)
